title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The DOM (Document Object Model) is a tree-structured, in-memory representation of an HTML document that JavaScript can read and modify.

Selecting elements:
• document.getElementById('id') — single element by ID.
• document.querySelector('css') — first match for a CSS selector.
• document.querySelectorAll('css') — NodeList of all matches.

Modifying elements:
• element.textContent — set/get text (safe — no HTML parsing).
• element.innerHTML — set/get HTML markup (risk of XSS if user input).
• element.setAttribute / getAttribute / removeAttribute
• element.classList.add / remove / toggle / contains
• element.style.property = 'value'

Creating and inserting:
• document.createElement('tag')
• parent.appendChild(child) / parent.prepend(child)
• element.remove()
